name: Birthday Cake Protein Cookies
description: Birthday-cake flavor of the Crumbl base — base chassis + almond extract for the cake-batter note. Funfetti sprinkles are the topping (omitted here, like the source). Derived off Crumbl Base Protein Cookie so base tweaks rebase in. Untested at this base chassis (the original standalone root was rated excellent, but this version adds the base's egg white / cornstarch and standardized dough — worth a confirming bake).

payload.text: 9 min is the ceiling, not the target — pull while centers look wet and underdone; they firm up completely as they cool on the pan. An extra minute here turns them dry and cakey.
The 20–30 min chill is what keeps these thick: cold dough spreads slowly and holds mounds instead of pooling flat. Don't skip it, especially if your kitchen is warm.
The almond extract goes in at 1/8 tsp — that's intentionally light. More than 1/4 tsp and the almond note overpowers the birthday-cake vanilla character.
Fold the Cool Whip in first before adding the protein and pudding powder — mixing them in while the base is still loose prevents lumps and keeps the frosting airy rather than dense.
Frost only once fully cooled; a warm cookie melts the yogurt frosting and it slides off. If you're making ahead, store cookies and frosting separately and assemble day-of.
